checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?
Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?

evidence.quote: OtherAssetsNoncurrent=143500000 (2010-05-31) vs OtherAssetsNoncurrent=171800000 (2011-02-28); PropertyPlantAndEquipmentNet=5465600000 (2010-05-31) vs PropertyPlantAndEquipmentNet=6330300000 (2011-02-28)

checklist.question: Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?
Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: GrossProfit=504700000 (2010-06-01 to 2010-08-31) on SalesRevenueGoodsNet=2188300000 (23.1% margin); GrossProfit=853600000 (2010-12-01 to 2011-02-28) on SalesRevenueGoodsNet=2214300000 (38.5% margin)

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss=100600000 (Q ending 2009-08-31); NetIncomeLoss=1025600000 (Q ending 2010-11-30); NetIncomeLoss=542100000 (Q ending 2011-02-28)

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: LiabilitiesCurrent=1303900000 (2010-05-31) vs LiabilitiesCurrent=1534500000 (2011-02-28); AccountsPayableCurrent=552500000 (2010-05-31) vs AccountsPayableCurrent=645400000 (2011-02-28)

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: 10-Q filed 2010-10-12 followed by 10-Q/A filed 2010-10-29 for the same period (2010-08-31)

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities only provided for two quarters: 2009-06-01/2009-08-31 and 2010-06-01/2010-08-31
Receivables, inventory, and payables series begin only at 2010-05-31, providing fewer than 4 comparable quarters and no full-year (10-K) figures

top_signals: Receivables-to-quarterly-revenue ratio jumped from ~24-25% (Aug/Nov 2010) to ~35% (Feb 2011): ReceivablesNetCurrent=777900000 vs SalesRevenueGoodsNet=2214300000 (2011-02-28 quarter)
A 10-Q/A was filed only 17 days after the original 10-Q for the quarter ended 2010-08-31 (filed 2010-10-12, amended 2010-10-29), and the company has a history of NT 10-Q/NT 10-K late-filing notices

Data coverage is thin (OCF given for only 2 of 6 quarters; balance-sheet series start only in mid-2010), limiting ability to confirm multi-period trends such as receivables/CFO divergence, though what data exists shows earnings volatility tracking revenue/margin swings consistent with the 2010-2011 fertilizer price cycle rather than smoothing
